svg.aria-label: Hold Your Fire data pipeline: a trajectory of thought-action-observation steps, sliced into prefixes that inherit the run's terminal label, turned into ~40 cheap structured features, scored by a 1.14 MB gradient-boosted model into a calibrated failure probability, then passed through an abstention gate that fires or stays silent
svg.text: What the monitor reads, and what it predicts

text.tspan: One agent run, sliced into prefixes, turned into ~40 cheap numbers, scored as a

svg.text: A sequence of steps. Each step is a (thought, action, observation) triple; the run ends with one success/fail label.
the run · instance: psf__requests-1142 · agent: swe-agent-llama
steps 3-6 repeat the same edit / test loop, and the test fail-count never drops
slice the run into growing prefixes
Each prefix is one training row
We score growing prefixes (steps 0..k). Every prefix inherits the run's terminal label, even early ones that still look healthy.
THE LABEL IS NOISY BY DESIGN
An early prefix of a failed run is labeled
FAIL even though nothing has gone wrong
yet. That irreducible early noise is why
the monitor learns to abstain, not guess.
prefix(k) = steps[:k] · n_total_steps is NOT a feature
extract cheap, structured features from the prefix
No raw code, no future info. The model never sees the patch, the outcome, or how long the run will be; just these counts and ratios.
highlighted = no-progress / repetition signals (what drives the risk up here)
A 1.14 MB gradient-boosted model maps the features to a risk in [0,1], then isotonic calibration makes it an honest probability.
decide whether there's enough evidence to act
A gate that knows when to stay silent
The monitor only fires when the prefix is deep enough and the risk is far enough from 0.5. Otherwise it abstains.
